Course objectives (Why should one study this course?) (about 100 words): Python is one of the most versatile and widely used scripting languages. It’s clean and uncluttered Syntax as well as its straightforward design greatly contribute to this success and make it an ideal language for programming education. Its application ranges from web development to scientific computing. Especially in the fields of data science and artificial intelligence, it is the most common programming language supported by all major data-handling and analytical frameworks. This course provides a thorough introduction to the language and its main features, as well as Insights into the rationale and application of important adjacent concepts such as environments, testing, and version control. Also in this course, students will learn how to code for building data science / machine learning applications. 12. Course outcomes (about 100 words): By the end of this course, students should: Understand Python syntax and programming concepts. ◆ Understand object-oriented concepts in Python. ◆ Analyze and apply different methods for error handling in Python. ◆ Understand important Python libraries related to data science and how to apply them ◆ to given programming tasks. Understand concepts like environments and version control. ◆ 13. (about 100 words): The objective of the course is to provide the fundamental knowledge of probability and random theory with an application to artificial intelligence and machine learning. 12. Course outcomes (about 100 words): By the end of this course, students should: ● Understand the concepts of random variables and random processes ● Comprehend the various statistical models used for modeling for machine learning application ● Apply the different detection and estimation rules in real time problems 13. Course contents: (Topics to appear as course contents in the Courses of Study booklet) (Include Practical / Practice activities): ● Fundamentals of Probability and Random Signal Theory ● Random Processes and Convergence ● Markov Chains and Applications ● Frequentist Statistics and Regression ● Bayesian Statistics and Hypothesis Testing 14. Moments, Generating multivariate random variables 2. ● Random Processes and Convergence 9 Definition, Stationarity of random processes, Mean and autocovariance functions, Independent identically-distributed sequences, Power spectral density, Gaussian process, Poisson process, Random walk, Convergence of Random Processes, Types of convergence, Law of large numbers, Central limit theorem, Monte Carlo simulation 3. ● Markov Chains and Applications 8 Time-homogeneous discrete-time Markov chains, Recurrence, Periodicity, Convergence, Markov-chain Monte Carlo, Descriptive statistics, Histogram, Sample mean and variance, Order statistics, Sample covariance, Sample covariance matrix 4. ● Frequentist Statistics and Regression 8 Independent identically-distributed sampling, Mean square error, Consistency, Condence intervals, Nonparametric model estimation, Parametric model estimation, Linear Regression models, Least-squares estimation, Overtting, Global warming. 5. ● Bayesian Statistics and Hypothesis Testing 8 Bayesian parametric models, Conjugate prior, Bayesian estimators, The hypothesis-testing framework, Parametric testing, Nonparametric testing: The permutation test, Multiple testing Total Lecture hours 42 15. (about 100 words): To develop semantic-based and context-aware systems to acquire, organize process, share and use the knowledge embedded in multimedia content. Research will aim to maximize automation of the complete knowledge lifecycle and achieve semantic interoperability between Web resources and services. The field of Robotics is a multi disciplinary as robots are amazingly complex system comprising mechanical, electrical, electronic H/W and S/W and issues germane to all these. 12. (about 100 words): This class deals with the fundamentals of characterizing and recognizing patterns and features of interest in numerical data. We also cover decision theory, statistical classification, maximum likelihood and Bayesian estimation, nonparametric methods, unsupervised learning and clustering. Additional topics on machine and human learning from active research are also talked about in the class 12. Course outcomes (about 100 words): This course will study state-of-the-art techniques for analyzing data. The goal is to extract meaningful information from feature data. This includes statistical and information theoretic concepts relating to pattern recognition, with applications. 13. Course contents: (Topics to appear as course contents in the Courses of Study booklet) (Include Practical / Practice activities): Bayes Decision Theory ◆ Parameter Estimation Methods ◆ Unsupervised learning and clustering ◆ Nonparametric techniques for density estimation ◆ Dimensionality reduction ◆ Linear discriminant functions ◆ Non-metric methods for pattern classification ◆ 14. Course objectives (Why should one study this course?) (about 100 words): Machine learning is a field of scientific study concerned with algorithmic techniques that enable machines to learn performance on a given task via the discovery of patterns or regularities in exemplary data. Consequently, its methods commonly draw upon a statistical basis in conjunction with the computational capabilities of modern computing hardware. This course aims to acquaint the student with the main branches of machine learning and provide a thorough introduction to the most widely used approaches and methods in this field. 12. Course outcomes (about 100 words): By the end of this course, students should: ● Know different machine learning model classes. ● Comprehend the difference between supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning methods. ● Understand common machine learning models. ● Analyze trade-offs in the application of different models. ● Appropriately choose machine learning models according to a given task. 13. (about 100 words): Neural networks and deep learning approaches have revolutionized the fields of data science and artificial intelligence in recent years, and applications built on these techniques have reached or surpassed human performance in many specialized applications. After a short review of the origins of neural networks and deep learning, this course will cover the most common neural network architectures and discuss in detail how neural networks are trained using dedicated data samples, avoiding common pitfalls such as overtraining. The course includes a detailed overview of alternative methods to train neural networks and further network architectures which are relevant in a wide range of specialized application scenarios. 12. (about 100 words): As data collection has increased exponentially, so has the need for people skilled at using and interacting with data; to be able to think critically, and provide insights to make better decisions and optimize their businesses. To work with data, we need to extract it from the database. This is where SQL comes into the picture. Relational Database Management is a crucial part of Data Science. This course is designed to give you a primer in the fundamentals of SQL and working with data so that you can begin analyzing it for data science purposes. You will begin to ask the right questions and come up with good answers to deliver valuable insights for your organization. This course starts with the basics and assumes you do not have any knowledge or skills in SQL. It will build on that foundation and gradually have you write both simple and complex queries to help you select data from tables. You'll start to work with different types of data like strings and numbers and discuss methods to filter and pare down your results. 12. Course outcomes (about 100 words): By the end of this course, students should: Identify SQL architecture, client/server relation, and database types ◆ Deploy several functions, operators, etc. for designing relational databases ◆ Modify data using T-SQL, views, and stored procedures ◆ Understand concept of triggers and their creation ◆ Utilize records for searching, sorting, indexing, and grouping ◆ Implement Database administration, types, and SQL Server tools and services ◆ Backup and restore databases ◆ 13. (about 100 words): Data are often considered the “new oil”, the raw material from which value is created. To harness the power of data, the data need to be stored and processed on a technical level. This course introduces the four “Vs” of data, as well as typical data sources and types, storing of data in HDFS & no-sql. Particular focus is given to database structures and different types of databases, e.g., relational, Nosql. Learn how the modern frameworks like Apache Spark & HDFS helps in solving complex problems like high volume data processing and high volume data storage. How to work with live streaming of huge data (Twitter or any other API) for data processing in real time. Learn how to use Apache spark in machine learning applications using MLlib. 12. Course outcomes (about 100 words): By the end of this course, students should: Identify different types and sources of data. ◆ Understand different database concepts. ◆ Learn to build new database structures. ◆ Evaluate various data storage frameworks w.r.t. Project requirements. ◆ Analyze which data format to use for a given project. ◆ Understand what roles you could take in such projects. ◆ Create a distributed computing environment for a given project. ◆ Understand the ethical impact of big data technology choices. ◆ 13. As the business world shifts towards a more digital operational framework, business infrastructure and organisational data face an enhanced risk of cyberattacks and violations. Cyber security has become an important factor for the success of the digital economy. As per KnowBe4’s ‘Phishing by Industry Benchmarking Report’, education, hospitality, and insurance are among the top industries that are at the risk in the small, medium, and large-sized organisations categories, respectively. The need for skilled cybersecurity professionals to prevent security breaches has increased, now more than ever. It is estimated that the Indian cyber security market will step-up to $35 billion (NASSCOM) by 2025 and there will be a 150% (Indeed.com) increase in demand for cyber security roles. # Page Title: Online Education | IIT Jammu

#### **Notification - Dated 06 February, 2025**

#### **Notification - Suspension of New batches of Executive/Online
PG Programs**

## Master of Technology in Artificial Intelligence and Machine
Learning (Executive)

**Program structure:**

1. Two year program consisting of 4 semesters. Refer to **Annexure
I** for details.
2. Program consists of 7 core courses and 3 elective courses with 42 hours
of live lectures each along with a capstone project and a dissertation.
Each course has a minimum 42 hours of lectures and tutorials over and
above.
3. All the lectures are offered online over the weekends (both Saturday and
Sunday).
4. Detailed course content for the courses carefully curated by the IIT
Jammu faculty are available as per **Annexure II**.
5. Provision for certificate (but not a degree) without Thesis is available.
6. If a candidate is unable to complete course requirement, there will be
provision for a summer semester to let him clear the backlog.

**Governing Committee:**

A governing committee consisting of in-house faculty and renowned industry
experts has been constituted to ensure compliance of the course with IIT Jammu
academic standards. This committee reports to the IIT Jammu senate at regular
intervals.

**Eligibility Criteria:**

Working professionals with minimum two years experience in industry having a
minimum of B. Tech/BE degree in any branch of Engineering or MCA or M.Sc. degree
in related areas.

**Enrolment Criterion:**

- Minimum CPI of 6.0 or 60% in qualifying degree, fulfilling specific
requirements for different disciplines.
- Qualifying criterion for professionals with more than 10 year experience
is relaxed to CPI of 5.5 or 55% marks.
- Shortlisted candidates will need  to clear a written test and/or
interview before enrolment.
- Students once enrolled will be provided a student id of IIT Jammu and can
access various services as availed by a regular student.

**Sales & marketing:**

M/s Intellipaat Software Solutions Pvt. Ltd. Bangalore has been roped in as our
partner for online sales & marketing, pre-selection of candidates as per our
norms, use of their online platform for delivery of content and exams as well as
fee collection.

**Admission Process**

1. Aspiring candidates should apply on the Intellipaat portal link https://intellipaat.com/m-tech-data-science-artificial-intelligence-iit-jammu/.
2. Intellipaat will verify the documents and may seek clarification
from candidates for any ambiguity and incompleteness. Intellipaat may
choose to ask for a fully refundable deposit along with application from
the candidate.
3. All applications will be reviewed by IIT Jammu and If an application is
not found to be prima facie meeting the required criterion, then IIT
Jammu will ask Intellipaat to reject the application and refund any
deposit given by the candidate in full.
4. Intellipaat will communicate to the eligible candidates, the date for
online proctored written test and interview after verification of
documents. The test and interview will be conducted on regular
intervals. Normally these will be scheduled on Saturdays and Sundays for
the convenience of candidates.
5. Selectin of a candidate will be done on the basis of his academic
credentials, experience and performance in written test and interview.
6. The list of selected candidates will be shared by IIT Jammu with
Intellipaat and also confirmation mail will be sent to selected
candidates by IIT Jammu. All those who are not selected, will be
refunded full deposit amount.
7. Offer letter for selected candidates will be sent by IIT Jammu thru email
and posts to candidates with copies to Intellipaat.
8. Intellipaat will initiate the collection of Balance fee from candidate
after offer letter has been sent by IIT Jammu.
9. Once payment as per terms has been received, Intellipaat will inform IIT
Jammu and enrolment letter containing all the details will be sent to
the candidate by IIT Jammu.
10. Contact details:

In case of any clarifications, kindly contact the course coordinator Dr. Karan
Nathwani on his email id karan.nathwani@iitjammu.ac.in.

For any query specific to admission process, kindly contact Admission Coordinator
Dr. Harkeerat Kaur on her email id harkeerat.kaur@iitjammu.ac.in.

For fees structure and payment and all other details kindly contact Intellipaat
at mtechadmissions.iitjammu@intellipaat.com.

## MTech in VLSI Design (Executive)

1. AI
2. Intelligent Systems
3. Sensors

**Introduction:**

At the forefront of technological innovation, the need for advanced expertise in
VLSI Design and Intelligent Systems is more pronounced than ever. With the tech
industry's rapid evolution and integration of AI with electronic design, IIT
Jammu introduces a ground-breaking program dedicated to moulding experts in
these intersecting domains. This program is tailored for those aspiring to
master the complexity of VLSI circuits and the ingenuity of intelligent systems
— a dual proficiency that's becoming indispensable in sectors like
telecommunications, smart electronics, and IoT.

The curriculum, designed in collaboration with industry leaders, combines
theoretical rigor with practical hands-on labs, real-world problem-solving,
preparing students to lead and innovate in a landscape where AI and VLSI design
are rewriting the rules of technological capability. This program by IIT Jammu
is more than an education; it's a direct conduit to becoming a tech
visionary in the high-demand field of intelligent electronic systems.

**Objectives:**

IIT Jammu's two-year M.Tech in VLSI Design is a pioneering academic venture
that marries the theoretical underpinnings of academia with the dynamic
pragmatism of the semiconductor, sensors and AI industries. This advanced
program is meticulously structured to navigate the intricate landscape of
microelectronics and smart technologies, fortifying students with the skills
needed to master complex integrated circuit design and intelligent system
innovation.

Emphasizing a dual focus, the program encompasses a comprehensive VLSI curriculum
that includes CMOS circuits, FPGA systems, and advanced electives like Edge AI
Computing Chips. Simultaneously, it delves into the realm of Intelligent
Systems, exploring the integration of AI with hardware design. The expanded
two-year timeline allows for an in-depth engagement with core subjects, a range
of electives, and a significant emphasis on practical application through
extensive project work and industry internships.

Graduates of this program, empowered by IIT Jammu's commitment to educational
excellence and interdisciplinary learning, will emerge as industry-ready experts
equipped for leading-edge roles in the rapidly evolving domains of VLSI and
intelligent system design.

**Program Highlights:**

1. Batch Start Date: September 2026 (Tentative)
2. Duration: 24 Months**
3. M.Tech by IIT Jammu
4. 60 academic credits post successful completion of the program
5. IIT Jammu Alumni Status post successful completion of the program
6. Visit IIT Jammu campus for 5-7 Days, every year for Immersion sessions and
examinations. The first year of campus immersion is mandatory, while the
second-year campus immersion is optional.
7. Live online sessions by IIT Jammu faculty, and industry experts.
8. 300+ Hours of Overall Learning Experience.
9. Multiple hours of online lab exercises and project work.

**After 1 Year of completion of course, candidate become eligible for PG Diploma
in VLSI Design subject to satisfactory performance.

**Learning Outcomes:**

1. Demonstrate expertise in state-of-the-art VLSI design and simulation tools
to develop, test, and optimize Digital and Analog circuits suitable for
various applications.
2. Design and evaluate integrated systems using advanced VLSI technologies,
applying knowledge of Solid-state devices and Semiconductor physics.
3. Develop embedded systems and programmable logic designs with FPGA,
integrating intelligent system functions for smart applications.
4. Utilize modern techniques in ASIC design flow, and manage VLSI projects from
concept to fabrication with an understanding of the complete semiconductor
ecosystem.
5. Incorporate AI and machine learning algorithms to enhance the capabilities
of VLSI designs, optimizing for performance and predictive maintenance.
6. Design cyber-physical systems and IoT devices by applying principles of VLSI
and intelligent systems, with a focus on security, efficiency, and
reliability.
7. Leverage advanced computer architecture and semiconductor memory
technologies to innovate and improve electronic device functionalities.
8. Apply knowledge of intelligent systems to VLSI platforms, enhancing edge
computing and hardware acceleration for robust, AI-driven applications.
9. Synthesize VLSI and intelligent system methodologies to create comprehensive
solutions for automotive electronics, IoT, and high-speed networking,
aligning with the rapid evolution of technology and industry demands.

**Campus Immersion Component:**

1. The campus immersion phase is a pivotal part of the program, offering
students the chance to engage in on-campus activities and sessions during
the designated immersion period. This allows participants to experience
traditional classroom settings and provides a platform to interact directly
with IIT Jammu's renowned faculty.
2. The first year of campus immersion is mandatory, while the second-year
campus immersion is optional.

## IIT Jammu, TimesPro offers Post Graduate Diploma in Cyber
Security

The Indian Institute of Technology Jammu announces its Post Graduate Diploma in
Cyber Security, a comprehensive 12-month program designed to equip individuals
with the necessary skills to excel in the field of cyber security. This program
combines theoretical knowledge, hands-on learning, and practical projects to
establish a strong foundation for a successful career in this rapidly evolving
domain.

The program encompasses a wide range of topics, including the design and
development of intrusion detection and prevention systems, security protocols
for low powered devices, email security, anti-phishing solutions, AI-powered
cyber defense, homomorphic encryption applications, infrastructure access
management solutions, security solutions for cloud-based virtualized resources,
VPNs and proxy development, PKI applications development, and much more.

This program is ideal for individuals with a background in Software Engineering
and IT, as well as those seeking a career shift into the field of cyber
security. IT and Engineering professionals aiming to enhance their knowledge in
secure technology design and development, system and network security analysis,
and application security will greatly benefit from this program.

Applicants must hold a qualifying degree in B.E, B.Tech, MCA, M.Sc./MS in IT, CS,
ECE, EE, or related fields. A minimum of one year of work experience in IT, CSE,
EC, EE, or related fields is required. The eligibility criteria for this program
are a minimum of 55% or 6 CGPA in the qualifying degree, while individuals with
over 10 years of work experience are eligible with an aggregate of 50% or 5.5
CGPA.

Admission to the program is based on relevant academic credentials and a
20-minute virtual interview conducted by an interview panel.

For more information and to apply for the program, please visit **https://bit.ly/3C6zb5K.**

#### **Brochure**

## M.Tech in GenAI and Data Science (Executive)

1. AI
2. Generative AI and Agentic Systems
3. Intelligent Systems
4. Data Science
5. LLMs

**Introduction:**

Across every industry, intelligence is no longer just
an advantage; it’s the foundation. From automation to autonomous systems,
the new era of AI demands engineers who can **build systems that think,
decide, and act on their own**. The world is moving from predictive
models to **Generative and Agentic systems**, AI that learns,
adapts, and operates autonomously. This shift has opened a massive opportunity
for engineers who can bridge code, cloud, and cognition. India today is entering
one of the most exciting phases in its growth story, as AI is reshaping how
companies operate, how governments deliver services, and how people live and
work.

With major national initiatives like **IndiaAI
Mission** and **BharatGen**, the country is creating
massive opportunities for skilled professionals who can design, deploy, and
manage AI systems at scale - where systems don’t just predict outcomes but
generate ideas, insights, and intelligent decisions.

Addressing India’s need of a new generation of
**AI specialists, engineers, and innovators** who can power this
transformation — the **M.Tech in Generative AI and Data Science
(Executive)** by IIT Jammu has been designed in collaboration with
industry leaders. It combines theoretical rigor with practical hands-on labs,
real-world problem-solving, preparing students to lead and innovate in a
landscape where AI is all pervasive. This program by IIT Jammu is more than an
education; it's a direct conduit to becoming a meaningful contributor to
India’s global AI mission.

**Objectives:**

The MTech in GenAI and Data Science (Executive) by IIT
Jammu is designed to develop next-generation AI engineers, data scientists, and
applied researchers who can build, deploy, and scale intelligent systems across
domains. Through a rigorous blend of foundational courses in programming, data
analytics, machine learning, and computational intelligence, learners will gain
a deep understanding of how algorithms, data, and models interact to create
real-world impact.

In advanced semesters, the focus shifts toward
Generative AI, Large Language Models, Agentic AI, and MLOps — enabling
students to design, optimize, and operationalize end-to-end AI pipelines. The
capstone dissertation provides hands-on experience in solving complex industrial
and societal challenges using AI-driven methods. Graduates emerge as technically
grounded, innovation-ready professionals equipped to lead AI transformation
across sectors.

**Program Highlights:**

1. **Batch Start Date**: September 2026 (Tentative)
2. **Duration**: 24 Months (4 Semesters, 60 Credits)
3. **Degree**: MTech in Generative AI & Data Science
(Executive) by IIT Jammu
4. **Credits**: 60 academic credits post successful completion
of the program
5. **Alumni Status**: IIT Jammu Executive Alumni Status post
successful completion of the program
6. **Campus Immersion**: The campus immersion phase is a
pivotal part of the program, offering students the chance to engage in
on-campus activities and sessions during the designated immersion
period. The first year of campus immersion is mandatory, while the
second-year campus immersion is optional.
7. **Faculty**: Live online sessions by IIT Jammu faculty, and
industry experts.
8. Learning Hours: 300+ Hours of guided learning + hands-on labs
9. **Capstone**: Dissertation I & II (24 credits)

**After 1 year of completion of the course, candidates will become eligible
for the PG Diploma in GenAI and Data Science:**

a) He/She has to complete **30 credits** at the end of the first
year.

b) To do this, we allow the student to take**additional 6 credits**
in the form of a project in the second semester.

**** A student can opt for the PG Diploma only after completion of the
first semester, not in the beginning of the course.**

**Program Objectives**

Following are some of the objectives of the program:

1. Build strong foundations in Data Science, Machine Learning, and Deep
Learning.
2. Develop expertise in Generative AI and Agentic workflows.
3. Enable professionals to apply AI to real-world Indian and global
challenges.
4. Strengthen implementation, deployment, and system integration skills.
5. Nurture ethical and responsible AI practice aligned with India’s AI
mission.

**Outcome of the Program**

At the end of the Program, the learners will have expertise in the following
areas:

**1. Technical Mastery in AI and Data Science**

Learners will:

1. Build strong foundations in **Mathematics, Statistics, and
Programming for AI and Data Science** through the Futurense
bridge modules.
2. Develop hands-on skills in **Python, SQL, and data
visualization** for structured and unstructured data.
3. Master key AI domains - **Machine Learning, Deep Learning, NLP,
Computer Vision, and Fuzzy Logic**- to design intelligent
models and pipelines.
4. Gain proficiency in **Generative AI, Agentic AI, and context-aware
systems**, including prompt engineering, RAG
(Retrieval-Augmented Generation), and agent orchestration.
5. Learn to design, train, and deploy AI models using modern tools such as
**PyTorch, TensorFlow, LangChain, Hugging Face, AutoGen, and
Spark.**

**2. Applied Innovation and System Building**

Learners will:

1. Move beyond model-building to **end-to-end AI system
design**, integrating data engineering, workflow automation,
and deployment (MLOps).
2. Apply **AI and Data Science** principles to solve complex
problems in domains like healthcare, fintech, governance, smart
manufacturing, and digital services.
3. Use **Big Data frameworks** (Apache Spark, Kafka, IoT) to
deploy large-scale, real-time data streams.
4. Implement **Generative and Agentic AI solutions** that
automate reasoning, decision-making, and content generation.
5. Translate research into production-grade systems through **capstone
projects and dissertations** in the third and fourth
semesters.

**3. Professional & Career Readiness**

Graduates will:

1. Earn **Executive Alumni Status from IIT Jammu**, providing
long-term career mobility and recognition.
2. Be equipped for **cross-functional AI roles** - from
technical engineering to solution architecture and AI product
management.
3. Learn to work with **distributed, cloud-based AI
environments**, leveraging MLOps, data pipelines, and ethical
deployment frameworks.
4. Build professional confidence through presentations, applied projects,
and **research-led dissertation work** aligned with
real-world use cases.
5. Contribute meaningfully towards building fair, inclusive, and socially
responsible **AI systems** aligned with the **AI for
Bharat vision.**

**Campus Immersion Component:**

1. The campus immersion phase is a pivotal part of the program, offering
students the chance to engage in on-campus activities and sessions
during the designated immersion period. This allows participants to
experience traditional classroom settings and provides a platform to
interact directly with IIT Jammu's renowned faculty.
2. Campus immersion is scheduled every academic year. The first-year campus
immersion is compulsory, while the second-year immersion is optional.

Please be aware of the following:

1. A detailed schedule for the campus immersion will be shared ahead of the
sessions.
2. Boarding and lodging expenses during the immersion shall be borne by the
learners.
3. Details regarding the issuance of learner identity cards will be
communicated prior to the immersion.

**Criteria of Eligibility:**

The eligibility criteria prescribed below are the absolute minimum with no
possible exceptions:

1. B.Tech / B.E. (ECE/EE/CSE/IT/EI/ME) / MSc. (IT/CS)
2. Minimum experience is 2 years (full time working experience post minimum
qualifications).
3. The qualifying criterion for admission to M.Tech (Executive) is CGPA of
6.0 or 60% in qualifying degree.
4. For candidates with more than 10 years of experience - Min CGPA
requirement is 5.5 or 55% in qualifying degree. (The applicant must
qualify the pre-screen test to be eligible for the program)

**Selection Process**

The program’s selection and nomination will be
based on the pre-assessment test followed by an online interview and submission
of required documents.

Upon passing the interview, the IIT Jammu Admission
team will review the application, documents, and exam score to ascertain the
selection followed by an offer letter.
